examples.title: Pass by Value - Square Function
examples.description: Demonstrates that pass by value creates a copy and does not modify the original variable
examples.explanation: The function receives a copy of 'num'. Even though x is squared inside the function, the original 'num' remains 5 because pass by value only works on a copy.

examples.title: Pass by Reference - Swap Function
examples.description: Demonstrates pass by reference using pointers to modify original variables
examples.explanation: The function receives addresses of x and y. Using pointers, it dereferences and swaps the values at those addresses. The original variables are modified.

examples.title: Array Passing to Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates that arrays are passed by address (pointer)
examples.explanation: When array is passed, only the base address is passed. The function receives a pointer to the first element. All modifications affect the original array since they share the same memory.

examples.title: Returning Multiple Values Using Pointers
examples.description: Demonstrates using pointers to return multiple values from a function
examples.explanation: C functions can return only one value using return statement. To return multiple values, we pass addresses of variables (pointers) and modify them inside the function.
